description: Transform LYNCKIWI (lead gen platform) into KYSS Vision (backpacker workforce placement platform). Read kyss_platform_flow_v2.md for business context, kyss_prd_v1.md for technical specs, and kyss_manus_transformation_prompt.md for file-by-file transformation instructions.

userStories.title: Cleanup: Delete all Lynck-specific files
userStories.description: Remove all files that are specific to Lynck and won't be reused. This gives us a clean foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete pages: QuoteRequest.tsx, ServiceDetail.tsx, ServiceDetailOld.tsx.backup, ThankYou.tsx, ForBusinesses.tsx, Advertise.tsx, AdvertiseCreate.tsx, AdminAds.tsx, AdminAdDetail.tsx, Impressum.tsx, About.tsx
Delete components: ServiceRequestForm.tsx, ads/AdCarousel.tsx, ads/AdSidebar.tsx, PartnerLogo.tsx, PortfolioGrid.tsx, blog/BlogCTA.tsx
Delete data files: servicesData.ts, landingPageContent.ts, priceRanges.ts, partnerLogos.ts, networkMetrics.ts
Delete lib files: ads.ts

Delete all Lynck images from public/ (blog-*.jpg, service-*.jpg, hero-house.png, logo.png, logo-old.png, electrician.jpg, heating-hvac.jpg, general-renovation.jpg, plumbing-sanitary.png, roofing.jpg, solar-panel.jpg)
Delete all root markdown docs: ALTERNATIVE_TASKS.md, DATABASE_SETUP.md, EDGE_FUNCTION_DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md, LEAD_QUALITY_IMPROVEMENTS.md, PHASE_*.md, SECURITY_FIXES_SUMMARY.md, NETLIFY_DEPLOYMENT.md
Delete supabase/functions/create-lead/ directory
Delete all files in supabase/migrations/
Project still compiles after removing unused imports from App.tsx (comment out deleted route imports for now)
Typecheck passes: npm run build
userStories.notes: First step — clean slate. App.tsx will have broken imports; comment them out temporarily. We'll rebuild routes in US-010.

userStories.title: Rebrand: Global find-and-replace Lynck to KYSS
userStories.description: Replace all Lynck branding with KYSS Vision branding across the entire codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: Find and replace globally: 'Lynck Services' → 'KYSS Vision', 'Lynck' → 'KYSS', 'LYNCK' → 'KYSS', 'lynck' → 'kyss'
Update index.html: title to 'KYSS Vision — Find Farm Work in New Zealand & Australia', meta description updated for KYSS
Update package.json: name to 'kyss-vision'
Update .env.example: variable names reference KYSS
Remove all German-language meta tags from index.html
Add placeholder favicon: create a simple gold circle SVG with 'K' as favicon
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Pure text replacement and branding. No structural changes.

userStories.title: Rebrand: Update Tailwind color system to gold/amber accent
userStories.description: Replace Lynck's green/emerald accent with KYSS gold/amber throughout the design system.
acceptanceCriteria: Update tailwind.config.ts: primary color changed to gold/amber (#F5A623 base, full ramp from 50-900)
Keep emerald (#10b981) as secondary color for verified badges and success states
Update any hardcoded green accent colors in src/index.css or src/App.css to gold/amber
Verify dark theme backgrounds remain as-is (#0a0a0a to #141414 range)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Color system only. The components will pick up new colors automatically via Tailwind config.

userStories.title: Remove German language system
userStories.description: Remove the DE/EN bilingual system. KYSS is English-only for MVP.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete src/contexts/LanguageContext.tsx
Remove LanguageProvider wrapper from App.tsx
Search all files for useLanguage hook calls and remove them, replacing with English-only strings
Remove language toggle from Navigation.tsx
Remove any { de: '...', en: '...' } structures — keep only the English value
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This will touch many files. Be thorough — search for 'useLanguage', 'LanguageContext', 'language', 'de:', 'nameDe', 'titleDe', 'descriptionDe'.

userStories.title: Database: Create KYSS Supabase schema — enums and core tables
userStories.description: Create the new Supabase migration with all KYSS enums and the core user/profile tables.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/001_kyss_schema.sql
Create all enums: user_role, user_status, visa_type, experience_level, accommodation_pref, pay_type, pool_status, membership_status, review_type, report_reason, report_status, notification_type
Create table: user_profiles (id, user_id references auth.users, role, name, nationality, profile_photo_url, country, phone, status, last_login_at, timestamps)
Create table: worker_profiles (all fields from PRD section 3 — visa, preferences, availability, emergency contact, profile_complete, manus_prospect_id)
Create table: employer_profiles (business_name, region, country, logo_url, description, verified, timestamps)
Create table: work_type_categories (name, slug unique, icon, description, display_order, active)
All tables have UUID primary keys with gen_random_uuid()
All tables have created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
Appropriate foreign keys and indexes created
Migration runs successfully against Supabase
userStories.notes: See kyss_prd_v1.md Section 3 for exact field definitions. Use PostgreSQL native types.

userStories.title: Database: Create work pools and pool membership tables
userStories.description: Create the work_pools and pool_memberships tables — the core engine of KYSS.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/002_pools_schema.sql

Create table: work_pools (all fields from PRD — employer_id, work_type_category_id, title, region, country, location, season dates, pay, hours, physical requirements, accommodation fields, transport fields, contract_url, induction_video_url, max_capacity, current_count, status, auto_close_at_capacity, created_by, timestamps)
Create table: pool_memberships (worker_id, pool_id, status enum, added_by nullable, joined_at, left_at, UNIQUE constraint on worker_id+pool_id)
Foreign keys: work_pools.employer_id → employer_profiles.id, work_pools.work_type_category_id → work_type_categories.id, work_pools.created_by → auth.users(id)
Foreign keys: pool_memberships.worker_id → auth.users(id), pool_memberships.pool_id → work_pools.id
Indexes on: work_pools(work_type_category_id), work_pools(employer_id), work_pools(status), work_pools(country), pool_memberships(worker_id), pool_memberships(pool_id)
Migration runs successfully
userStories.notes: See PRD Section 3 for exact field types. pool_status enum: open, almost_full, closed, archived.

userStories.title: Database: Create communication tables (feed, messages, notifications)
userStories.description: Create tables for pool feed posts, messaging, and notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/003_communication_schema.sql
Create table: pool_posts (pool_id, author_id, content text max 2000, pinned boolean, timestamps)
Create table: pool_post_comments (post_id, author_id, content text max 500, timestamps)
Create table: conversations (participant_ids uuid[], pool_id nullable, last_message_at, timestamps)
Create table: messages (conversation_id, sender_id, content text max 2000, read boolean default false, timestamps)
Create table: notifications (user_id, type notification_type enum, title, body, read boolean default false, data jsonb, timestamps)
All foreign keys and indexes created
Migration runs successfully

userStories.title: Database: Create reviews, reports, admin log, and prospects tables
userStories.description: Create remaining tables for reviews, safety, admin tracking, and Manus pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/004_remaining_schema.sql
Create table: reviews (reviewer_id, reviewee_id, pool_id, review_type enum, rating 1-5, text max 1000, UNIQUE on reviewer_id+pool_id, timestamps)
Create table: reports (reporter_id, reported_id, reason enum, description, status enum, admin_notes, actioned_by, timestamps)
Create table: admin_actions (admin_id, action_type text, target_type text, target_id uuid, details jsonb, timestamps)

Create table: prospects (name, facebook_url, source_group, nationality, visa_confirmed, start_date_confirmed, work_type_interest, region_interest, dm_sent, dm_sent_at, dm_replied, dm_replied_at, platform_signed_up, signed_up_at, user_id nullable, pool_joined, pool_joined_at, notes, timestamps)
Migration runs successfully

userStories.title: Database: Seed work type categories and create RLS policies
userStories.description: Insert the 9 work type categories and set up Row Level Security.
acceptanceCriteria: Create supabase/migrations/005_seed_and_rls.sql

Insert 9 work type categories: Fruit Picking, Packing & Processing, Pruning, Thinning, Vineyard, Dairy & Farming, Hospitality, Construction, Horticulture — with slugs, icons, descriptions, and display_order 1-9
Enable RLS on ALL tables
Create RLS policies: workers can read own profile, update own profile
Workers can read work_type_categories and work_pools (where status != 'archived')
Workers can insert pool_memberships for themselves (join pools)
Workers can read pool_posts and comments for pools they are members of
Employers can read/update their own employer_profile
Employers can read pool_memberships for their own pools
Employers can create pool_posts for their own pools
Admin role can read/write all tables (create a policy using user_profiles.role = 'admin')
Public can read work_type_categories (for landing page)
Migration runs successfully
userStories.notes: RLS is critical for security. Test that unauthorized access is properly blocked.

userStories.title: Regenerate Supabase types and update client
userStories.description: Update the Supabase TypeScript types to match the new schema.
acceptanceCriteria: Regenerate src/integrations/supabase/types.ts from new schema (run supabase gen types or manually update the type file to match all new tables)
Verify src/integrations/supabase/client.ts still works with new types
Export all new table types for use in components
npm run build passes (may have import errors from deleted files — fix any remaining broken imports in App.tsx)
userStories.notes: If you can't run supabase gen types, manually write the TypeScript types matching the SQL schema.

userStories.title: Auth: Create AuthContext with three-role support
userStories.description: Replace LanguageContext with AuthContext that supports worker/employer/admin roles.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx
Provides: user (from Supabase auth), userProfile (from user_profiles table), role ('worker'|'employer'|'admin'|null), isLoading, isAuthenticated
Provides functions: signIn(email, password), signUp(email, password, name, role, country), signOut()
On auth state change, fetch user_profiles row to get role
signUp creates both auth.users entry AND user_profiles row with role
Wrap app with AuthProvider in App.tsx (replacing old LanguageProvider)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation for all protected routes. Keep it clean and simple.

userStories.title: Auth: Create Sign Up page
userStories.description: Build the sign-up page for workers and employers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/auth/SignUp.tsx
Form fields: Full name, Email, Password (min 8 chars), Country (NZ/AU/Both radio), Role (Worker/Employer radio, default Worker), Terms checkbox
Client-side validation on all fields
On submit: calls AuthContext.signUp, handles errors, shows loading state
After successful sign-up: redirect worker to /profile/edit, redirect employer to /employer/profile
Link to sign-in page: 'Already have an account? Sign in'
Uses existing shadcn/ui form components (Input, RadioGroup, Checkbox, Button)
Dark theme, gold accent on primary CTA button
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Auth: Create Sign In page and update ProtectedRoute
userStories.description: Build the sign-in page and update route protection for three roles.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/auth/SignIn.tsx (replaces AdminLogin.tsx functionality)
Form fields: Email, Password, [Sign In] button
After sign-in: redirect based on role — worker → /dashboard, employer → /employer/dashboard, admin → /admin
Link to sign-up: 'Don't have an account? Get started'
Update src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx to accept requiredRole prop
ProtectedRoute checks: if not authenticated → redirect to /auth/sign-in
ProtectedRoute checks: if authenticated but wrong role → redirect to correct dashboard
Delete AdminLogin.tsx
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Data: Create work type categories data file and region data
userStories.description: Create the static data files that replace Lynck's servicesData.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/data/workTypeCategoriesData.ts with WorkTypeCategory type and array of 9 categories (id, slug, name, icon, description, imagePath)
Create src/data/regionsData.ts with nzRegions (16 regions) and auRegions (8 regions) arrays

Create src/data/landingContent.ts with all KYSS landing page copy in English: hero headlines/subheadlines, trust badges, features/why-kyss content, how-it-works 4 steps, 6 testimonials, stats, CTA content
Export all types and data
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: See kyss_manus_transformation_prompt.md Phase 7 for exact content. See kyss_transformation_blueprint.md for testimonial text.

userStories.title: Lib: Rewrite database.ts with KYSS functions
userStories.description: Replace all Lynck database functions with KYSS-specific Supabase queries.
acceptanceCriteria: Completely rewrite src/lib/database.ts
Add functions for categories: getCategories(), getCategoryBySlug(slug), getCategoryPoolCounts(country?)
Add functions for pools: getPools(filters), getPoolsByCategory(categorySlug, filters), getPoolById(poolId), joinPool(poolId), leavePool(poolId), getRecommendedPools(userId)
Add functions for pool feed: getPoolPosts(poolId), createPoolPost(poolId, content), pinPoolPost(postId, pinned)
Add functions for worker profile: getWorkerProfile(userId), updateWorkerProfile(userId, data), checkProfileComplete(userId)
Add functions for employer: getEmployerProfile(userId), getEmployerPools(employerId)
Add functions for messaging: getConversations(userId), getMessages(conversationId), sendMessage(conversationId, content)
Add functions for notifications: getNotifications(userId), markNotificationRead(id), getUnreadCount(userId)

Add admin functions: adminGetWorkers(filters), adminGetPools(filters), adminCreatePool(data), adminUpdatePoolStatus(poolId, status), adminAddWorkerToPool(workerId, poolId), adminRemoveWorkerFromPool(workerId, poolId), adminGetDashboardStats()
All functions use the Supabase client from src/integrations/supabase/client.ts
All functions have proper TypeScript return types
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is a large file but it's all Supabase query functions. No UI. See PRD Section 5 for the full API endpoint list — each endpoint maps to a function here.

userStories.title: Lib: Update auth.ts and create categoryIcons.tsx
userStories.description: Update auth utilities and create icon mapping for work type categories.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/lib/auth.ts: signIn, signUp (creates auth user + user_profiles row), signOut, getCurrentUser, getUserRole functions using Supabase Auth
Rename src/lib/serviceIcons.tsx to src/lib/categoryIcons.tsx
Map work type category icon names to lucide-react icons: apple, package, scissors, leaf, grape, milk (or cow equivalent), hotel, hard-hat, sprout
Export a getCategoryIcon(iconName: string) function that returns the React component
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Component: Transform Navigation for public/worker/employer views
userStories.description: Update the main navigation component to handle three states: public, worker logged in, employer logged in.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/Navigation.tsx
PUBLIC state (not logged in): KYSS Logo | How It Works | Guide | Blog | For Employers | [Sign In] text button | [Get Started →] gold filled button
WORKER state (logged in): KYSS Logo | Search | Guide | Blog | Notification bell with unread count badge | Profile avatar dropdown (Dashboard, My Pools, Messages, Settings, Sign Out)
EMPLOYER state (logged in): KYSS Logo | Search | Notification bell | Profile avatar dropdown (Dashboard, Pool Management, Messages, Settings, Sign Out)
ADMIN state: minimal nav — admin uses sidebar layout instead
Uses AuthContext to determine current state
Mobile responsive: hamburger menu on small screens
Dark theme with gold accent on active items and CTA buttons
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Use existing shadcn/ui NavigationMenu, DropdownMenu, and Sheet components.

userStories.title: Component: Transform Footer for KYSS
userStories.description: Update the footer with KYSS content and links.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/Footer.tsx
4 columns: KYSS Vision + tagline | Work Types (9 links) | Company (About, Blog, Guide, For Employers, Contact) | Legal (Privacy, Terms, Cookies)
Countries row: NZ flag + 'New Zealand' | AU flag + 'Australia'
Social links: Instagram, TikTok, Facebook (use lucide-react icons)
Copyright: '© 2026 KYSS Vision. All rights reserved.'
Dark theme consistent with existing footer styling
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Component: Transform HeroSection for KYSS landing page
userStories.description: Rebuild the hero section for the KYSS landing page.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/HeroSection.tsx
Headline: 'Find Farm Work Across New Zealand & Australia'
Subheadline: 'Join verified backpackers already matched with seasonal employers. Safe, fast, and built for travelers.'
Two gold CTA buttons: '🇳🇿 Explore New Zealand' and '🇦🇺 Explore Australia' — both link to /auth/sign-up with country query param
Trust badges row: 'Verified Employers' | '100% Free for Workers' | '24h Average Match Time' — with checkmark/shield/clock icons
Background: use a placeholder gradient or solid dark color (no image required for now)
Remove all Lynck-specific content (house image, service icons overlay, contractor copy)
Dark theme, responsive
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Component: Transform FeaturesSection and WhyChooseUs
userStories.description: Update the 'Why KYSS?' and 'How It Works' sections for the landing page.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/FeaturesSection.tsx as 'Why KYSS?' section with 3 value props: Verified Employers (checkmark icon), Built for Safety (shield icon), Instant Matching (zap icon) — each with title and description text from landingContent.ts

Rewrite src/components/WhyChooseUs.tsx as 'How It Works' section with 4 numbered step cards: Create Your Profile, Browse Work Pools, Join a Pool, Start Working — keep the numbered card layout from Lynck but update content
Both sections use gold accent for icons/numbers
Dark theme, responsive
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Component: Transform TestimonialsSection for KYSS
userStories.description: Update testimonials with KYSS backpacker and employer reviews.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/TestimonialsSection.tsx
6 review cards: 3 from workers (Sophie L./France, Kenji T./Japan, Laura M./Germany) and 3 from employers (Bay Gold Orchards, Hawke's Bay Vineyards, Suncoast Farms)
Each card: initials avatar (colored circle), name, role + location, 5 star rating, review text, gold tag (e.g., 'Matched in 48 hours', '40 Workers Hired')
Keep the existing card grid layout, swap green tags for gold tags
Stats bar below testimonials: 'Workers Matched' | 'Verified Employers' | 'Avg Rating' | 'Avg Match Time'
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Use testimonial content from kyss_transformation_blueprint.md

userStories.title: Component: Create CategoryCard and CategoryGrid for work type display
userStories.description: Build the work type category card component used on landing page and find-work page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/categories/CategoryCard.tsx — displays: icon (from categoryIcons), name, description, open pool count badge, total pool count. Click navigates to /find-work/[slug] (if logged in) or /auth/sign-up (if not)
Create src/components/categories/CategoryGrid.tsx — renders 3x3 grid of CategoryCards (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
Cards use dark theme styling (#1a1a1a bg, #2a2a2a border, rounded-xl, gold accent on hover)
Pool counts are passed as props (will be fetched from DB later, use 0 as default)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This component is reused in both the public landing page (/work types section) and the logged-in /find-work page.

userStories.title: Component: Create ContactSection (CTA) for KYSS
userStories.description: Update the bottom CTA section of the landing page.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/components/ContactSection.tsx
Headline: 'Ready to Start Your Next Adventure?'
Subtitle: 'Join thousands of backpackers finding safe, verified seasonal work.'
Two CTA buttons: [Create Your Free Profile →] gold primary | [I'm an Employer →] outlined secondary
Both link to /auth/sign-up (with role query param for employer)
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Page: Rebuild Index.tsx (landing page) with all KYSS sections
userStories.description: Assemble the complete KYSS landing page from transformed components.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/pages/Index.tsx

Sections in order: Navigation → HeroSection → CategoryGrid (work types, using static data for now) → FeaturesSection (Why KYSS) → WhyChooseUs (How It Works) → TestimonialsSection → ContactSection (CTA) → Footer
Remove all Lynck-specific sections (ad sidebar, service icon overlays, etc.)
Page is fully responsive and looks polished in dark theme
All internal links work (/auth/sign-up, /blog, /guide, etc. — pages may not exist yet, but links should be correct)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is the public-facing marketing page. It should look professional and complete.

userStories.title: Routing: Set up complete App.tsx route structure
userStories.description: Configure all routes for public, worker, employer, and admin sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite src/App.tsx with the complete route structure from kyss_manus_transformation_prompt.md Phase 5
Public routes: /, /how-it-works, /for-employers, /guide, /blog, /blog/:slug, /work/:categorySlug, /auth/sign-up, /auth/sign-in, /privacy, /terms, /cookies
Worker routes (ProtectedRoute requiredRole='worker'): /dashboard, /find-work, /find-work/:categorySlug, /pool/:poolId, /my-pools, /messages, /notifications, /profile, /profile/edit, /settings
Employer routes (ProtectedRoute requiredRole='employer'): /employer/dashboard, /employer/pool/:poolId, /employer/messages, /employer/notifications, /employer/profile, /employer/settings

Admin routes (ProtectedRoute requiredRole='admin'): /admin, /admin/workers, /admin/workers/:id, /admin/employers, /admin/pools, /admin/pools/create, /admin/pools/:id, /admin/flagged, /admin/analytics/*, /admin/prospects, /admin/activity-log, /admin/blog, /admin/guide
All routes lazy-loaded with Suspense and PageLoader fallback
Create placeholder pages for routes that don't have real pages yet (simple component with page title)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Create minimal placeholder components for pages not yet built. We'll flesh them out in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Layout: Create WorkerSidebar component
userStories.description: Build the sidebar navigation for logged-in workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/WorkerSidebar.tsx

Sections: MAIN (Dashboard, Find Work, My Pools with count badge, Messages with unread dot, Notifications with count), RESOURCES (Guide, Blog, Seasonal Calendar), MY POOLS (list of joined pools with quick links), bottom (Profile, Settings, Sign Out)
Active item: gold/amber pill highlight background with dark text
Hover: subtle lighter background
Icons: use lucide-react (Home, Search, Clipboard, MessageSquare, Bell, BookOpen, PenTool, Calendar, User, Settings, LogOut)
Collapsible on mobile (slide-in overlay triggered by hamburger menu)
Dark theme (#0d0d0d to #141414 bg, light gray text, muted labels)
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Reference the NovaCast sidebar screenshot for styling. Gold active state is key.

userStories.title: Layout: Create WorkerLayout and EmployerLayout wrapper components
userStories.description: Build layout wrappers that include sidebar + header for logged-in users.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/WorkerLayout.tsx — sidebar (WorkerSidebar) on left + main content area on right + top nav (simplified Navigation with bell + avatar)
Create src/components/EmployerSidebar.tsx — employer-specific sidebar (Dashboard, My Pools, Messages, Notifications, Pool Management section, Profile, Settings, Sign Out)
Create src/components/EmployerLayout.tsx — EmployerSidebar + main content area
Both layouts responsive: sidebar hidden on mobile, toggled via hamburger menu
Main content area scrollable, sidebar fixed
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Layout: Update AdminLayout and AdminSidebar for KYSS
userStories.description: Transform the existing admin layout to match KYSS admin sidebar spec.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/components/AdminLayout.tsx
Update or replace src/components/Sidebar.tsx with AdminSidebar

Admin sidebar sections: OVERVIEW (Dashboard), MANAGEMENT (Workers with count, Employers with count, Work Pools with count, Flagged Content with count), ANALYTICS (Workers, Employers, Pools, Funnel, Revenue), TOOLS (Bulk Messaging, Prospect Pipeline, Activity Log), CONTENT (Blog Manager, Guide Editor), bottom (Platform Settings, Sign Out)
Same styling pattern as WorkerSidebar (dark theme, gold active state)
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Component: Create PoolCard for pool listings
userStories.description: Build the pool card component used in category listings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/pools/PoolCard.tsx

Displays: status badge (green OPEN / amber ALMOST FULL / red FULL), employer name, region, employer rating (stars + count), pay rate + type, hours, accommodation indicator, transport indicator, capacity progress bar (X/Y spots filled), [View & Join Pool →] button (for open) or [View Pool] grayed out (for closed)
Create src/components/pools/StatusBadge.tsx — colored badge for pool status
Create src/components/pools/CapacityBar.tsx — visual progress bar showing filled/total spots
Dark card styling, responsive
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Page: Build FindWork page (work type category grid)
userStories.description: Create the main 'Find Work' page showing the 9 work type category cards.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/worker/FindWork.tsx
Access control: redirect to /profile/edit if worker profile is not complete
Country toggle at top: NZ | AU | Both (defaults to worker's country setting)
Uses CategoryGrid component to display 9 work type category cards
Each card shows live pool counts fetched from Supabase (open pools in that category for selected country)
Wrapped in WorkerLayout (sidebar + nav)
Page title: 'Find Work' with subtitle 'Choose a work type to see available pools'
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is the first page workers see after completing their profile.

userStories.title: Page: Build CategoryPools page (pools within a category)
userStories.description: Create the page showing all pools within a selected work type category.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/worker/CategoryPools.tsx
Reads :categorySlug from URL params
Back button: '← Back to Work Types' → /find-work
Category title + icon + description at top
Country toggle: NZ | AU | Both
Filters: Region (multi-select), Pay range (slider), Accommodation (Yes/No/Any), Transport (Yes/No/Any), Status (Open & Almost Full / All)
Sort dropdown: Relevance (default), Newest, Pay highest, Most spots
Pool list: OPEN pools first, then ALMOST_FULL, then CLOSED (grayed out)
Uses PoolCard component for each pool
Fetches pools from Supabase filtered by category
Wrapped in WorkerLayout
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is the critical navigation step: Category → Pools. Open pools joinable, closed pools visible but grayed out.

userStories.title: Page: Build PoolDetail page with About tab
userStories.description: Create the pool detail page — the core page where workers view pool info and join.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/worker/PoolDetail.tsx
Pool header: employer logo + name, region, status badge, capacity bar, employer rating, [JOIN THIS POOL] gold button
If worker already a member: show '✅ You're in this pool' + [Leave Pool] link
If pool closed: show 'This pool is currently full' (no button)

Tab 1 — About (default): work type, season dates, pay rate, hours, physical requirements, accommodation section (available/details/cost), transport section (available/details/cost), documents ([Download Contract] if exists, [Watch Induction Video] if exists), employer description
Tabs 2 (Feed) and 3 (Reviews) can be placeholder content for now
Fetches pool data from Supabase by pool ID
Wrapped in WorkerLayout
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: The join button logic will be implemented in the next story.

userStories.title: Feature: Implement pool join and leave flow
userStories.description: Build the join/leave logic with all validations from the PRD.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/pools/PoolJoinModal.tsx — confirmation dialog showing pool name, work type, start date, pay rate, and confirm/cancel buttons
On confirm: create pool_membership (status: active, added_by: null), increment pool.current_count, check capacity thresholds (90% → almost_full, 100% → closed if auto_close)
Validations before join: profile must be complete, visa not expired, visa expiry >= pool.season_start, not already a member, pool must be open or almost_full
Show appropriate error messages for each validation failure
Leave flow: confirmation dialog → update membership status to 'left', decrement pool.current_count, recalculate pool status
After join: redirect to pool page with 'You're in this pool' state
Create notification for employer on join: 'New worker: [name] joined [pool]'
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: See PRD Section 7 for all edge cases and validation rules. Use database-level locking for race condition protection.

userStories.title: Page: Build Pool Feed tab
userStories.description: Implement the pool feed tab where employers post updates and workers comment.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Feed tab content to PoolDetail.tsx
Fetch pool_posts for this pool, ordered by pinned first then newest
Each post shows: author avatar + name, timestamp, content, pin indicator, comment count
Expand comments inline on click (newest first)
Workers can add comments (text input, max 500 chars, submit button)
Only pool members can see and interact with the feed
Non-members see: 'Join this pool to access the feed'
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Page: Build Pool Reviews tab
userStories.description: Implement the reviews tab on the pool detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Reviews tab content to PoolDetail.tsx
Fetch reviews for this pool where review_type = 'worker_rates_employer'
Show average rating at top (only if 3+ reviews, otherwise 'Not enough reviews yet')
Each review: reviewer name, nationality flag, star rating (1-5), review text, date
If pool season is completed: show 'Leave a review' button for members who haven't reviewed yet
Review form: 1-5 star selector + text area (max 1000 chars) + submit
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Page: Build Worker Profile Wizard (6-step form)
userStories.description: Create the multi-step profile completion wizard for new workers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/worker/ProfileWizard.tsx
Progress bar at top showing current step and completion %
Step 1 — Personal Info: nationality dropdown, phone, languages multi-select
Step 2 — Visa: visa type dropdown, file upload (PDF/JPG/PNG, max 10MB), expiry date picker. Validation: expiry must be future
Step 3 — Work Preferences: work type checkboxes (from categories), region multi-select (filtered by country), availability start/end dates, experience level radio
Step 4 — Logistics: own transport (Yes/No), accommodation preference radio
Step 5 — Photo & Bio: profile photo upload (JPG/PNG, max 5MB), bio textarea (max 500 chars)
Step 6 — Emergency Contact: name, phone with country code, relationship dropdown
Each step auto-saves on 'Next' (updates worker_profiles in Supabase)
On final submit: set profile_complete = true, redirect to /find-work
Success toast: 'Profile complete! Browse open work pools below.'
Uses shadcn/ui form components throughout
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: This is a large page. Use a step state machine (useState for currentStep). Each step is a sub-component.

userStories.title: Page: Build Worker Dashboard
userStories.description: Create the main dashboard workers see after logging in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/pages/worker/Dashboard.tsx
Top row — 3 stat cards: Pools Joined (count), Unread Messages (count), Profile Status (✅ Complete or progress %)
Section: Recommended Pools — 3-5 PoolCards matching worker preferences, only open/almost_full pools they haven't joined
Section: My Pool Updates — latest 5 pool feed posts from joined pools (combined feed, newest first)
Section: Recent Messages — 3 most recent conversations with unread indicator
Wrapped in WorkerLayout
npm run build passes

No broken images (placeholder images where real ones aren't available)
